Sometimes, a scenario may require you to make a test at the start of each turn, or pause to determine if a victory condition has been achieved before playing on. These things are also done now, before the turn begins in earnest. This sub-phase should also be used as a moment in which to think between turns, during which you can remove stray casualties, errant dice and other bits of gaming detritus that have accumulated. Often, players will have questions to ask their opponent, such as how a special rule works or what a magic item does. spells.name: Skitterleap
spells.flavour: With a puff of smoke and a loud pop of inrushing air, the Skaven Wizard disappears, only to reappear elsewhere on the battlefield.
spells.effect: This spell can only target friendly characters whose Troop Type is infantry , but may target characters engaged in combat. You may immediately remove the target friendly character from the battlefield and replace it anywhere completely within 24" of its original location, but not within 6" of any enemy models. The target cannot move again during this Movement phase . Note that this spell allows a character to leave combat.

spells.name: Warp Lightning
spells.flavour: The Skaven Wizard points a fleshy paw, from which bolts of greenish-black lightning arc outwards.
spells.effect: The target enemy unit suffers D3+1 Strength 5 hits, each with an AP of -3.

spells.name: Cloud of Corruption
spells.flavour: With a flourish of their palsied paws, the Skaven Wizard summons forth a stinking cloud of foul contagion.
spells.effect: Remains in Play . Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is within 9" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template is treated as dangerous terrain over which no line of sight can be drawn. Whilst within 3" of one or more of these templates, enemy units suffer a -1 modifier to their Toughness characteristic (to a minimum of one). , Switch Index Links Credit Sitemap Support Report Bug {"props":{"pageProps":{"entry":{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"5ayc3Mq9J0yzy7li0PD0XY","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-02-16T02:46:30.130Z","updatedAt":"2024-02-16T02:46:38.868Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":11,"revision":2,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"rule"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"name":"Lore of the Horned Rat","slug":"lore-of-the-horned-rat","pageReference":24,"order":10,"body":{"nodeType":"document","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Skaven believe all magic originates from the same source, their powerful and fickle god – the Horned Rat. In the following sections, you will find the Lores of Magic – lists from which Wizards generate their spells. Each Lore of Magic represents a particular approach towards the study and use of magic, giving each a particular character reflected by the spells within it. Different Wizards have access to different Lores of Magic. Every Wizard's rules include the Lore(s) of Magic they know. In some cases, it will be stated that they know spells from a single, given Lore of Magic, whilst in others it will be stated that they know spells from one of a number of given Lores of Magic. Where this is the case, you must choose one of these Lores when writing your muster list."}}],"association":[{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"1ajj5ld8tacvR3LPTz6t5S"}}],"bodyIndex":"Each player randomly generates spells for each of their Wizards before armies are deployed. If your army contains more than one Wizard, you may generate spells for each in an order of your choosing. Each Lore of Magic contains seven spells: six numbered (1-6) and a seventh 'signature' spell. To determine the spells your Wizard knows, roll a number of D6 equal to their Level of Wizardry, re-rolling any duplicate results. The result(s) show which spell(s) your Wizard knows. If you wish, your Wizard may discard one of these randomly generated spells and instead select the signature spell of their chosen Lore of Magic. Any number of Wizards in your army may do this. Note that Wizards in the same army may know the same spell(s), so individual spells may be duplicated within your army. However, no single Wizard can know the same spell twice."}}},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"signature spell","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" of their chosen ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"entry-hyperlink","data":{"target":{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"edGlqDa8R0B8jQO0Ho314F","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-01-16T15:16:36.485Z","updatedAt":"2024-02-02T02:32:30.884Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":10,"revision":4,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"ruleType"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"name":"The Lores of Magic","slug":"the-lores-of-magic","pageReference":319,"order":290,"body":{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Wizards are a formidable force on the battlefield, able to wreak incredible destruction, weaken or strengthen other warriors, or summon terrible beasts to fight at their side. A Magical Vortex does not require a target – instead, it uses a round template which is placed on the battlefield, not touching the bases of any models, and with its central hole within the range given by the spell. Once placed, Magical Vortexes remain on the battlefield (unless they move off it). Some Magical Vortexes move at the start of each turn, often by scattering. Should a Magical Vortex end such a move over a unit, move it by the smallest amount possible, in any direction, so that it can be placed on the battlefield, not touching the bases of any models."}}},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Magical Vortex","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-row","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Casting Value","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"10+","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-row","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Range","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"9\"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"entry-hyperlink","data":{"target":{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"47I7wEK8VTs953Q1H6Q9e6","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-01-24T02:35:20.646Z","updatedAt":"2024-02-08T00:13:53.881Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":9,"revision":5,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"rule"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"name":"Remains in Play","slug":"remains-in-play","pageReference":111,"order":320,"body":{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Some spells are marked 'Remains in Play'. Particularly dense and dangerous woods or steep and rock strewn hills can also be counted as dangerous terrain.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Dangerous terrain hinders movement just like difficult terrain. In addition, any model (on its own or within a unit) that either begins its movement in dangerous terrain, passes through dangerous terrain during its movement, or ends its movement in dangerous terrain must make a Dangerous Terrain test. To make a Dangerous Terrain test, roll a D6. On a roll of 2+, the model successfully negotiates the dangers of the terrain without incident. On a roll of 1, the model has suffered a terrible mishap and loses a single Wound.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"For the purposes of combat, dangerous terrain is treated as difficult terrain.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"Note that a model must make a Dangerous Terrain test for each separate dangerous terrain feature it encounters during a single move.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"document"},"relatedLinks":[{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"dlfz6d4LoClc9Wkf438jm6"}}],"ruleType":[{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"sB44C1sX5J6c7CQrHwZfOK"}}],"association":[{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"1ajj5ld8tacvR3LPTz6t5S"}}],"bodyIndex":"Some terrain can present a very real risk to life and limb. This might include particularly rocky ground, deep swamps, fast flowing rivers, and even unnatural or magical terrain features. Particularly dense and dangerous woods or steep and rock strewn hills can also be counted as dangerous terrain. Dangerous terrain hinders movement just like difficult terrain. In addition, any model (on its own or within a unit) that either begins its movement in dangerous terrain, passes through dangerous terrain during its movement, or ends its movement in dangerous terrain must make a Dangerous Terrain test. To make a Dangerous Terrain test, roll a D6. On a roll of 2+, the model successfully negotiates the dangers of the terrain without incident. On a roll of 1, the model has suffered a terrible mishap and loses a single Wound. For the purposes of combat, dangerous terrain is treated as difficult terrain. Note that a model must make a Dangerous Terrain test for each separate dangerous terrain feature it encounters during a single move."}}},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"dangerous terrain","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" over which no line of sight can be drawn. Where this is the case, you must choose one of these Lores when writing your muster list.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"document"},"bodyIndex":"Wizards are a formidable force on the battlefield, able to wreak incredible destruction, weaken or strengthen other warriors, or summon terrible beasts to fight at their side. The rules for using Wizards and casting spells in your games have already been covered in detail in the Magic section. In the following sections, you will find the Lores of Magic – lists from which Wizards generate their spells. Each Lore of Magic represents a particular approach towards the study and use of magic, giving each a particular character reflected by the spells within it. Different Wizards have access to different Lores of Magic. Every Wizard's rules include the Lore(s) of Magic they know. In some cases, it will be stated that they know spells from a single, given Lore of Magic, whilst in others it will be stated that they know spells from one of a number of given Lores of Magic. Where this is the case, you must choose one of these Lores when writing your muster list."}}],"association":[{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"3Q8T4uoOa6u7LvOOKDjKFa","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-02-02T18:22:41.287Z","updatedAt":"2024-09-26T12:55:41.605Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":22,"revision":9,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"association"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"abbreviation":"SK","name":"Skaven","slug":"skaven","order":220,"file":"https://warcomprod2024productionstorage.s3.eu-west-2.amazonaws.com/warhammertheoldworld_legends_skaven_eng_24.09-e6x1ehem8p.pdf","fileLastModifiedDate":"2024-03-21T10:13+00:00","fileLastVersionNumber":1.1,"displayDownloadLink":true,"useArmyListEntry":true,"status":"3"}}],"bodyIndex":"Skaven believe all magic originates from the same source, their powerful and fickle god – the Horned Rat. In truth, the potent and destructive magic of the Skaven relies upon the manipulation of the Winds of Magic, without which even the most devout and cunning Skaven would be unable to weave the simplest spell. A Wizard with the 'Lore of the Horned Rat' special rule may discard one of their randomly generated spells as normal. When they do so, they may select instead either the signature spell of their chosen Lore of Magic, or one of the spells listed below. spells.name: The Lady's Gift
spells.flavour: As the Damsel prays solemnly to the Lady of the Lake, silvery light emanates from her most ardent followers, knitting their wounds and gilding their armour.
spells.effect: 7+ /10+ Remains in Play . If this spell is cast with a casting result of 7 or more, the target friendly unit gains the Regeneration (6+) special rule. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 10 or more, the target friendly unit gains the Regeneration (5+) special rule. This spell may target a friendly unit engaged in combat.

spells.name: Burning Gaze
spells.flavour: Beams of cleansing white light flow from the Damsel's eyes, searing all traces of evil from her sight.
spells.effect: Draw a straight line, 5D6" in length, from the caster's base edge. Any enemy model whose base falls under this line suffers a single Strength 4 hit with no armour save permitted ( Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal).

spells.name: The Lady's Wrath
spells.flavour: Enraged to see the lands of fair Bretonnia invaded by enemies, the Damsel invokes the wrath of the Lady of the Lake, strengthening the sword arms of her companions.
spells.effect: Remains in Play . Whilst this spell is in play, the caster, their mount and any unit they have joined gain a +1 modifier to their Strength characteristic (to a maximum of 10), and improve the Armour Piercing characteristic of their weapons by 1. , Switch Index Links Credit Sitemap Support Report Bug {"props":{"pageProps":{"entry":{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"4hC4IPRVKwA8o9HpoeyFfD","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-02-10T04:11:40.293Z","updatedAt":"2024-02-10T04:19:28.932Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":16,"revision":3,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"rule"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"name":"Lore of the Lady","slug":"lore-of-the-lady","pageReference":109,"order":10,"body":{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"The magical powers of the Handmaidens of the Lady all resemble religious observance as much as they do sorcery. To determine the spells your Wizard knows, roll a number of D6 equal to their Level of Wizardry, re-rolling any duplicate results. The result(s) show which spell(s) your Wizard knows. If you wish, your Wizard may discard one of these randomly generated spells and instead select the signature spell of their chosen Lore of Magic. Any number of Wizards in your army may do this. Note that Wizards in the same army may know the same spell(s), so individual spells may be duplicated within your army. In the following sections, you will find the Lores of Magic – lists from which Wizards generate their spells.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Each Lore of Magic represents a particular approach towards the study and use of magic, giving each a particular character reflected by the spells within it. Different Wizards have access to different Lores of Magic.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Every Wizard's rules include the Lore(s) of Magic they know. In some cases, it will be stated that they know spells from a single, given Lore of Magic, whilst in others it will be stated that they know spells from one of a number of given Lores of Magic. Where this is the case, you must choose one of these Lores when writing your muster list.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"document"},"bodyIndex":"Wizards are a formidable force on the battlefield, able to wreak incredible destruction, weaken or strengthen other warriors, or summon terrible beasts to fight at their side. The rules for using Wizards and casting spells in your games have already been covered in detail in the Magic section. In the following sections, you will find the Lores of Magic – lists from which Wizards generate their spells. Each Lore of Magic represents a particular approach towards the study and use of magic, giving each a particular character reflected by the spells within it. Different Wizards have access to different Lores of Magic. Every Wizard's rules include the Lore(s) of Magic they know. In some cases, it will be stated that they know spells from a single, given Lore of Magic, whilst in others it will be stated that they know spells from one of a number of given Lores of Magic. Where this is the case, you must choose one of these Lores when writing your muster list."}}],"association":[{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"Pr1j2CwFi8QxERaKAHUNd","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-02-02T19:48:22.043Z","updatedAt":"2024-02-18T20:25:18.800Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":4,"revision":2,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"association"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"abbreviation":"FoF","name":"Forces of Fantasy","slug":"forces-of-fantasy","order":20,"hidden":["Yes"]}},{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"3yCDMYNiN3Fm3SLyCxvVWc","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-02-02T18:17:10.859Z","updatedAt":"2024-02-23T03:30:00.626Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":12,"revision":6,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"association"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"abbreviation":"KoB","name":"Kingdom of Bretonnia","slug":"kingdom-of-bretonnia","order":180,"useArmyListEntry":true,"status":"3"}}],"bodyIndex":"The magical powers of the Handmaidens of the Lady all resemble religious observance as much as they do sorcery. With hands clasped in devotion and rapturous joy upon her brow, a Damsel or Prophetess beseeches her goddess to protect and empower her dutiful followers. A Wizard with the 'Lore of the Lady' special rule may discard one of their randomly generated spells as normal. When they do so, they may select instead either the signature spell of their chosen Lore of Magic, or one of the spells listed below. "}},"crossReference":{"errata":[{"fields":{"name":"Page 109 - Burning Gaze","slug":"page-109-burning-gaze"},"sys":{"id":"25mpwE7eBZML1BU7nYee2M","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"errata"}},"type":"Entry"}}],"magicItem":[{"fields":{"name":"The Staff of the Elements","slug":"the-staff-of-the-elements"},"sys":{"id":"12oS5k2JtR9448iO4smPv4","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"magicItem"}},"type":"Entry"}}],"unit":[{"fields":{"name":"Damsel","slug":"damsel"},"sys":{"id":"5NxvnEBKRpBwz5NYDA59oq","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"armyListEntry"}},"type":"Entry"}},{"fields":{"name":"Lady Élisse Duchaard","slug":"lady-elisse-duchaard"},"sys":{"id":"6Xpc7cTjxtfY1unscUGkUW","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"armyListEntry"}},"type":"Entry"}},{"fields":{"name":"Prophetess","slug":"prophetess"},"sys":{"id":"5bMXBFUkn52cBlVrBcfqSx","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"armyListEntry"}},"type":"Entry"}}]},"prev":null,"next":null},"__N_SSG":true},"page":"/[ruleType]/[rule]","query":{"ruleType":"the-lores-of-magic","rule":"lore-of-the-lady"},"buildId":"N4Z7YDen35qO_9XViV8IP","isFallback":false,"gsp":true,"scriptLoader":[]}

spells.name: Maelstrom of Chaos
spells.flavour: The Sorcerer intones words of the Dark Tongue, summoning the breath of the gods from the dark skies above and gathering it about them before unleashing it upon their enemies.
spells.effect: Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is directly over the centre of the target enemy unit. Once placed, the template will scatter D3+1". Any enemy model whose base lies underneath the template risks being hit and suffering a single Strength 4 hit with an AP of -3.

spells.name: Blackened Bolts
spells.flavour: With a gesture, the Sorcerer turns the tendrils of shadow that crawl about them into bolts of dark lightning.
spells.effect: The target enemy unit suffers 2D6 Strength 3 hits, each with an AP of -1. Any unit that suffers one or more unsaved wounds from this spell must make a Panic test as if it had taken heavy casualties.

spells.name: Veil of Gloom
spells.flavour: Shrouded by the lingering darkness of the Shadowlands, the Sorcerer summons the breath of the gods to dissipate the enemy's attacks.
spells.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target friendly unit gains a 4+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by a template , and a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered in the Shooting phase .

spells.name: Vortex of Darkness
spells.flavour: A swirling tornado of screaming souls and inky-darkness rends and tears at those it passes, shredding flesh and spirit alike.
spells.effect: Remains in Play . Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is within 15" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template is treated as difficult terrain . The template scatters D6" during every Start of Turn sub-phase. Any unit (friend or foe) that moves through the template, or that the template moves over, suffers 3D6 Strength 2 hits, with no armour saves allowed ( Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal).

spells.name: Shadowed Assailants
spells.flavour: At the Sorcerer's command, the enemy's own shadows turn upon them, clawing at flesh with talons of darkness.
spells.effect: Combat A single enemy unit the caster is engaged in combat with suffers 3D6 Strength 1 hits, with no armour or Regeneration save permitted ( Ward saves can be attempted as normal).

spells.name: Crawling Mists
spells.flavour: The Sorcerer manipulates the cloying fog of the Shadowlands to cloak the movements of their allies and hasten their advance.
spells.effect: 6+ /9+ If this spell is cast with a casting result of 6 or more, a single friendly unit within 6" of the caster gains the Reserve Move special rule. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 9 or more, every friendly unit within 6" of the caster gains the Reserve Move special rule. This spell lasts until the end of this turn.

spells.name: Chains of Darkness
spells.flavour: Shadowy ichor materialises about the foe, solidifying into blackened shackles that weigh heavy upon their limbs.
spells.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target enemy unit suffers a -1 modifier to its Movement (to a minimum of 1) and Leadership characteristics (to a minimum of 2). Previous - Lore of Chaos

spells.name: Swirling Mists
spells.flavour: The beguiling mist that has turned about many travellers can be called upon to shroud the forces of Athel Loren from prying eyes.
spells.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, any enemy model that directs a shooting attack at the caster, or any unit they have joined, must re-roll any rolls To Hit of a natural 6.

spells.name: Fury of Athel Loren
spells.flavour: The caster summons forth writhing vines and briars from the ground to slash and strangle those who seek to harm Athel Loren and its inhabitants.
spells.effect: The target enemy unit suffers D3+1 Strength 6 hits, each with an AP of -2 and the Multiple Wounds (2) special rule.

spells.name: Ariel's Blessing
spells.flavour: Channelling the power of the Mage Queen herself, the caster surrounds their allies in a healing golden light.
spells.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target friendly unit gains the Regeneration (5+) special rule.

spells.name: Spiteful Torrent
spells.flavour: Swarms of Spites and forest insects erupt from the casters' staff, racing across the battlefield as a writhing mass that bites and stings all within reach.
spells.effect: Remains in Play . Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is within 15" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template is treated as dangerous terrain . The template scatters D6" during every Start of Turn sub-phase. Any enemy unit that moves through the template, or that the template moves over, suffers 3D6 Strength 2 hits, each with an AP of -1.

spells.name: Durthu's Wrath
spells.flavour: Having witnessed untold destruction within his homeland, Durthu's rage echoes through Athel Loren and can be drawn upon by those who act in harmony with the forest.
spells.effect: Combat Each model in the fighting rank of a unit that the caster is engaged with must immediately make an Initiative test. Each model that fails this test suffers D3 Strength 4 hits, with no armour save permitted ( Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal).

spells.name: Hidden Pathways
spells.flavour: The forests of Athel Loren can be compelled to hurry the passage of those who act with its best interests at heart.
spells.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target friendly unit gains the Reserve Move special rule and may perform a Quick Turn , even if it would not normally be able to do so.

spells.name: Sapping Blight
spells.flavour: As roots draw water from the earth, so too can the mages of Athel Loren sap the lifeforce from their foes.
spells.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target enemy unit suffers a -D3 modifier to its Strength characteristic (to a minimum of 1). This spell may target an enemy unit engaged in combat. If this spell is cast, the effects of any other Hex previously cast on the target unit immediately expire. , Previous - Lore of Athel Loren

spells.name: Big Smartz
spells.flavour: Gesturing lazily with a crooked staff, the caster enhances and sharpens the minds of those nearby, giving them a clearer look upon the world.
spells.effect: Remains in Play . Whilst this spell is in play, friendly units within the Command range of this model may re-roll any failed Stupidity tests. Additionally, if a friendly unit within the Command range of this model when this spell is cast failed its Stupidity test during the Start of Turn sub-phase of the same turn, it may immediately make this test again.

spells.name: Acidic Bile
spells.flavour: Spitting a guttural incantation, the caster launches a ball of sickly green and yellow Troll vomit toward the enemy, scorching flesh and dissolving armour.
spells.effect: Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is directly over the centre of the target enemy unit. Once placed, the template will scatter D3+1". Any enemy model whose base lies underneath the template's final position risks being hit and suffering a Strength 3 hit with an AP of -2.

spells.name: Troll Brainz
spells.flavour: Under the beguiling influence of this crude magic, even the most experienced warriors can quickly find themselves hopelessly confused and at a loss for what to do.
spells.effect: Remains in Play . Whilst this spell is in play, the target enemy unit becomes subject to the Stupidity special rule and reduces their Leadership characteristic by 1.

spells.name: Ravenous Recourse
spells.flavour: Through arcane means or by simply being reminded how hungry they are, the assembled Trolls race forward with a sudden burst of vigour.
spells.effect: Until the end of this turn, all friendly units that have the Stupidity special rule and are within 12" of the caster gain a +2 modifier to their Movement characteristic.

spells.name: Foetid Whirlpool
spells.flavour: A deluge of foul swamp water and unmentionable waste is spun into a swirling tornado and unleashed onto the battlefield.
spells.effect: Remains in Play . Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is within 18" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template is treated as difficult terrain . The template scatters D6" during every Start of Turn sub-phase. Any enemy unit that moves through the template, or that the template moves over, suffers D3+3 Strength 4 hits, each with an AP of -3.

spells.name: Torrent of Filth
spells.flavour: With an almighty belch, the caster heaves a stream of burning bile across their foes as the battlelines clash.
spells.effect: Combat Place a flame template so that the narrow end touches the caster's base edge and the broad end is over a unit they are engaged in combat with. Any model (friend or foe) whose base lies underneath the template risks being hit and suffering a single Strength 3 hit with an AP of -2.

spells.name: Rapid Regeneration
spells.flavour: Drawing on the regenerative properties of theTrolls, the caster channels that enhanced healing towards those in harm's way.
spells.effect: Until the end of your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target friendly unit gains the Flammable and Regeneration (5+) special rules. , Previous - Lore of Mork

spells.name: Vanhal's Danse Macabre
spells.flavour: Filled with magical energy, the Undead jerk forwards on the attack with tireless and unnatural speed.
spells.effect: 8+ / 12+ If this spell is cast with a casting result of 8 or more, the target friendly unit gains a +D3 modifier to one of the following characteristics (to a maximum of 10). If this spell is cast with a casting result of 12 or more, the target friendly unit gains a +D3 modifier to two of the following characteristics (to a maximum of 10). This spell lasts until your next Start of Turn sub-phase. Movement Weapon Skill Initiative

spells.name: Hellish Vigour
spells.flavour: The caster invigorates their ghastly warriors, who advance upon the foe with uncanny vigour.
spells.effect: 7 +/10+ If this spell is cast with a casting result of 7 or more, a single friendly unit that has the Necromantic Undead special rule and is within the caster's Command range gains the Reserve Move special rule. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 10 or more, every friendly unit that has the Necromantic Undead special rule and is within the caster's Command range gains the Reserve Move special rule. This spell lasts until the end of this turn.

spells.name: Raise Dead
spells.flavour: One skilled in the dark arts of Necromancy can awaken mortal remains to reinforce the Undead legions under their control.
spells.effect: Place a unit of 2D3 Risen Zombies on the battlefield anywhere completely within 12" of this model, but not within 1" of any enemy models. This unit cannot declare a charge during the turn in which it was raised: Troop Type: Regular Infantry Base Size: 25 x 25 mm Unit Size: 2D3 Equipment: Hand weapons Special Rules: Necromantic Undead , Regeneration (6+) , Skirmishers , The Newly Dead Note that Risen Zombies are not worth any Victory Points . , Switch Index Links Credit Sitemap Support Report Bug {"props":{"pageProps":{"entry":{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"4SqyPnQW5IVUBV1qeh1Ikc","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-02-16T04:03:26.753Z","updatedAt":"2024-02-16T04:04:19.122Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":9,"revision":2,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"rule"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"name":"Lore of Undeath","slug":"lore-of-undeath","pageReference":29,"order":10,"body":{"nodeType":"document","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Mystery shrouds the study of Necromancy. To learn this dark art, an aspirant must find a willing tutor and become their apprentice, or acquire forbidden books rich in the secrets of undeath. It is this intrinsic mystery that drives Necromancers to become servants of the Vampire Counts, hoping to learn first-hand from the masters of undeath.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"A Wizard with the 'Lore of Undeath' special rule may discard one of their randomly generated spells as normal. In the following sections, you will find the Lores of Magic – lists from which Wizards generate their spells.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Each Lore of Magic represents a particular approach towards the study and use of magic, giving each a particular character reflected by the spells within it. Where this is the case, you must choose one of these Lores when writing your muster list.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"document"},"bodyIndex":"Wizards are a formidable force on the battlefield, able to wreak incredible destruction, weaken or strengthen other warriors, or summon terrible beasts to fight at their side. The rules for using Wizards and casting spells in your games have already been covered in detail in the Magic section. In the following sections, you will find the Lores of Magic – lists from which Wizards generate their spells. Each Lore of Magic represents a particular approach towards the study and use of magic, giving each a particular character reflected by the spells within it. Different Wizards have access to different Lores of Magic. Every Wizard's rules include the Lore(s) of Magic they know. In some cases, it will be stated that they know spells from a single, given Lore of Magic, whilst in others it will be stated that they know spells from one of a number of given Lores of Magic. Where this is the case, you must choose one of these Lores when writing your muster list."}}],"association":[{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"1ajj5ld8tacvR3LPTz6t5S"}}],"bodyIndex":"Each player randomly generates spells for each of their Wizards before armies are deployed. If your army contains more than one Wizard, you may generate spells for each in an order of your choosing. Each Lore of Magic contains seven spells: six numbered (1-6) and a seventh 'signature' spell. To determine the spells your Wizard knows, roll a number of D6 equal to their Level of Wizardry, re-rolling any duplicate results. The result(s) show which spell(s) your Wizard knows. If you wish, your Wizard may discard one of these randomly generated spells and instead select the signature spell of their chosen Lore of Magic. Any number of Wizards in your army may do this. Note that Wizards in the same army may know the same spell(s), so individual spells may be duplicated within your army. However, no single Wizard can know the same spell twice."}}},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"signature spell","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" of their chosen ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"entry-hyperlink","data":{"target":{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"edGlqDa8R0B8jQO0Ho314F","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-01-16T15:16:36.485Z","updatedAt":"2024-02-02T02:32:30.884Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":10,"revision":4,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"ruleType"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"name":"The Lores of Magic","slug":"the-lores-of-magic","pageReference":319,"order":290,"body":{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Wizards are a formidable force on the battlefield, able to wreak incredible destruction, weaken or strengthen other warriors, or summon terrible beasts to fight at their side. The rules for using Wizards and casting spells in your games have already been covered in detail in the Magic section. In the following sections, you will find the Lores of Magic – lists from which Wizards generate their spells. Each Lore of Magic represents a particular approach towards the study and use of magic, giving each a particular character reflected by the spells within it. Different Wizards have access to different Lores of Magic. Every Wizard's rules include the Lore(s) of Magic they know. In some cases, it will be stated that they know spells from a single, given Lore of Magic, whilst in others it will be stated that they know spells from one of a number of given Lores of Magic. Where this is the case, you must choose one of these Lores when writing your muster list."}}},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Lore of In order to win, one player must score at least 100 Victory Points more than their opponent – if one player scores twice as many Victory Points as their opponent, that player has achieved a crushing victory! Any other result is a draw. Victory Points are won in several ways: Dead or Fled The King is Dead Trophies of War Scenario Objectives Special Features "}}},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Victory Points","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":".","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"data":{}}]}]},"type":"Signature Spell"},"sys":{"id":"cukhdS4XG5owL8gd44ZRk0","type":"Entry"}}]},"content":[]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},"ruleType":[{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"edGlqDa8R0B8jQO0Ho314F","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-01-16T15:16:36.485Z","updatedAt":"2024-02-02T02:32:30.884Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":10,"revision":4,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"ruleType"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"name":"The Lores of Magic","slug":"the-lores-of-magic","pageReference":319,"order":290,"body":{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Wizards are a formidable force on the battlefield, able to wreak incredible destruction, weaken or strengthen other warriors, or summon terrible beasts to fight at their side. The rules for using Wizards and casting spells in your games have already been covered in detail in the ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"target":{"sys":{"id":"iu5UDPnR5CzBKA1GTdRIiq","type":"Link","linkType":"Entry"}}},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Magic","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"entry-hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" section. In the following sections, you will find the Lores of Magic – lists from which Wizards generate their spells.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Each Lore of Magic represents a particular approach towards the study and use of magic, giving each a particular character reflected by the spells within it. Different Wizards have access to different Lores of Magic.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Every Wizard's rules include the Lore(s) of Magic they know. In some cases, it will be stated that they know spells from a single, given Lore of Magic, whilst in others it will be stated that they know spells from one of a number of given Lores of Magic. Where this is the case, you must choose one of these Lores when writing your muster list.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"document"},"bodyIndex":"Wizards are a formidable force on the battlefield, able to wreak incredible destruction, weaken or strengthen other warriors, or summon terrible beasts to fight at their side. The rules for using Wizards and casting spells in your games have already been covered in detail in the Magic section. In the following sections, you will find the Lores of Magic – lists from which Wizards generate their spells. Each Lore of Magic represents a particular approach towards the study and use of magic, giving each a particular character reflected by the spells within it. Different Wizards have access to different Lores of Magic. Every Wizard's rules include the Lore(s) of Magic they know. In some cases, it will be stated that they know spells from a single, given Lore of Magic, whilst in others it will be stated that they know spells from one of a number of given Lores of Magic. Where this is the case, you must choose one of these Lores when writing your muster list."}}],"association":[{"metadata":{"tags":[],"concepts":[]},"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"0v8db9sevlqj"}},"id":"BMGv7b2M2th9fQuRELDTE","type":"Entry","createdAt":"2024-02-02T18:24:15.936Z","updatedAt":"2024-09-26T12:55:46.933Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment"}},"publishedVersion":25,"revision":10,"contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"association"}},"locale":"en-US"},"fields":{"abbreviation":"VC","name":"Vampire Counts","slug":"vampire-counts","order":240,"file":"https://warcomprod2024productionstorage.s3.eu-west-2.amazonaws.com/warhammertheoldworld_legends_vampirecounts_eng_24.09-541v4zzg64.pdf","fileLastModifiedDate":"2024-03-21T10:11+00:00","fileLastVersionNumber":1.1,"displayDownloadLink":true,"useArmyListEntry":true,"status":"3"}}],"bodyIndex":"Mystery shrouds the study of Necromancy. To learn this dark art, an aspirant must find a willing tutor and become their apprentice, or acquire forbidden books rich in the secrets of undeath. It is this intrinsic mystery that drives Necromancers to become servants of the Vampire Counts, hoping to learn first-hand from the masters of undeath. A Wizard with the 'Lore of Undeath' special rule may discard one of their randomly generated spells as normal. When they do so, they may select instead either the signature spell of their chosen Lore of Magic, or one of the spells listed below. "}},"crossReference":{"unit":[{"fields":{"name":"Corpse Cart","slug":"corpse-cart"},"sys":{"id":"1dfZiJqgk58Ur1S2E1YNde","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"armyListEntry"}},"type":"Entry"}},{"fields":{"name":"Master Necromancer","slug":"master-necromancer"},"sys":{"id":"6OvkPYXY7JnVj0y8yzQpv8","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"armyListEntry"}},"type":"Entry"}},{"fields":{"name":"Necromantic Acolyte","slug":"necromantic-acolyte"},"sys":{"id":"3BUJ3uNcoli1TKS4hn4IO1","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"armyListEntry"}},"type":"Entry"}},{"fields":{"name":"Strigoi Ghoul King","slug":"strigoi-ghoul-king"},"sys":{"id":"7ny88Vj95QGwDK6vgr3WRX","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"armyListEntry"}},"type":"Entry"}},{"fields":{"name":"Vampire Count","slug":"vampire-count"},"sys":{"id":"GxQxLK9hnG4ynh9ovXVUv","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"armyListEntry"}},"type":"Entry"}},{"fields":{"name":"Vampire Thrall","slug":"vampire-thrall"},"sys":{"id":"1f1kAzWsWqY0T4K7226MT8","contentType":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"ContentType","id":"armyListEntry"}},"type":"Entry"}}]},"prev":null,"next":null},"__N_SSG":true},"page":"/[ruleType]/[rule]","query":{"ruleType":"the-lores-of-magic","rule":"lore-of-undeath"},"buildId":"N4Z7YDen35qO_9XViV8IP","isFallback":false,"gsp":true,"scriptLoader":[]}

spells.name: Constellation of the Dragon
spells.flavour: Blazing starlight leaps from the Wizard's fingertips, dancing and forming a simulacrum of the constellation of the Dragon Emperor above the battlefield.
spells.effect: 7+/11+ If this spell is cast with a casting result of 7 or more, the target enemy unit suffers D6+1 Strength 4 hits, each with an AP of -. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 11 or more, the target enemy unit suffers 2D6+2 Strength 4 hits, each with an AP of -1.

spells.name: Great Bastion
spells.flavour: With a word of power from the Wizard, the earth cracks and buckles as it is transformed into ramparts upon which gleaming battlements burn with celestial flame.
spells.effect: Remains in Play . The target friendly unit gains a 6+ Ward save against any wounds suffered. In addition, every model within the front rank of an enemy unit that ends its charge move in base contact with the target unit whilst this spell is in play must make a Dangerous Terrain test. Any Wounds lost are counted as 'unsaved wounds inflicted' when calculating the combat result. However, whilst this spell is in play the target unit cannot march or charge . This spell may target a friendly unit engaged in combat.

spells.name: Might of Heaven & Earth
spells.flavour: Calling upon the elemental energies of earth and heaven, the Wizard and those around them become imbued with the pure power of the Yang.
spells.effect: 9+/12+ If this spell is cast with a casting result of 9 or more, the caster, their mount and any unit they have joined, gain a +1 modifier to their Weapon Skill and Strength characteristics (to a maximum of 10), and gain the Flaming Attacks special rule. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 12 or more, all friendly units that are within the caster's Command range when this spell is cast gain a + 1 modifier to their Weapon Skill and Strength characteristics (to a maximum of 10), and gain the Flaming Attacks special rule. This spell lasts until the end of this turn. , Next - Lore of Yin

spells.name: Spirits of Wind & Shadow
spells.flavour: Elemental spirits descend upon the foe, enveloping them in a whirlwind of flickering shapes and ghostly gales.
spells.effect: As they try to advance, those afflicted find themselves waylaid by the grasping talons of unseen spirits and buffeted by spectral winds. Casting Value Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target enemy unit becomes subject to the Random Movement (D6) special rule.

spells.name: Accursed Mirror
spells.flavour: The Wizard creates a magical barrier of shifting darkness and howling wind around the enemy. This barrier snatches the enemy's missiles from the air and swallows the tips of their spears, instantly turning the lethal power of these weapons back upon their bearers.
spells.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, any rolls To Hit of a natural 1 made by the target enemy unit during either the Shooting phase or the Combat phase result in a Strength 3 hit with an AP of -1 which must be resolved against the target enemy unit. This spell may target an enemy unit engaged in combat.

spells.name: Ancestral Warriors
spells.flavour: Assailment
spells.effect: 7+/11+ Combat If this spell is cast with a casting result of 7 or more, a single enemy unit the caster is engaged in combat with suffers 2D3 Strength 2 hits, each with an AP of -. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 11 or more, the target enemy unit suffers 2D6 Strength 4 hits, each with an AP of -1. This spell has the Armour Bane (2) special rule. , Previous - Lore of Yang

spells.name: The Dwellers Below
spells.flavour: Beneath the enemy the cold dirt parts, revealing spectral hands that reach to grasp at the foe and pull them into the underworld.
spells.effect: Combat Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is directly over the centre of a unit the caster is engaged in combat with. Once placed, the template will scatter D3+1". Any enemy model whose base lies underneath the template's final position risks being hit and suffering a single Strength 3 hit with an AP of -.

spells.name: Deathly Cabal
spells.flavour: From beyond the veil of death, unquiet spirits gather protectively around the Necromancer, their presence causing the foe to recoil in fear and dread.
spells.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the caster and any unit they have joined gain a 6+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by a non-magical enemy attack. In addition, whilst this spell is in play, the affected models gain the Fear special rule. If they already have the Fear special rule, they instead gain the Terror special rule.

spells.name: Unquiet Spirits
spells.flavour: Evil spirits gather around the enemy, their spectral talons creeping beneath armour to tear at flesh.
spells.effect: The target enemy unit suffers 3D6 Strength 2 hits, with no armour save permitted ( Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal).

spells.name: Spiritual Vortex
spells.flavour: The Necromancer gestures and dark energy swirls, opening a vortex into the realm of Morr, god of death.
spells.effect: Remains in Play . Place a large (5") blast template so that its central hole is within 12" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template does not move and is treated as dangerous terrain . Whilst within 8" of one or more Spiritual Vortexes, enemy units suffer a -1 modifier to their Leadership characteristic (to a minimum of 2) and cannot use their General's Inspiring Presence special rule.

spells.name: Curse of Years
spells.flavour: As dead things draw near, they feed hungrily upon the vital energy of the living, ageing them rapidly.
spells.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target enemy unit suffers a -1 modifier to its Movement, Weapon Skill and Toughness characteristics (to a minimum of 1). If this spell is cast, the effects of any other Hex previously cast on the target unit immediately expire.

spells.name: Spectral Steed
spells.flavour: As the Necromancer chants words of dark power, a ghostly steed forms from the night air.
spells.effect: Remains in Play . This spell can only target friendly characters whose troop type is infantry . Whilst this spell is in play, the target friendly character gains the Ethereal and Fly (10) special rules.

spells.name: Spirit Leech
spells.flavour: Chill winds gust and long shadows gather, sapping the enemy's will and sowing seeds of dread.
spells.effect: Until the end of this turn, the target enemy unit suffers a -2 modifier to its Leadership characteristic (to a minimum of 2) and cannot use their General's Inspiring Presence special rule. This spell may target an enemy unit engaged in combat. , Previous - Illusion Next - Waaagh! Magic

spells.name: Fist of Gork (or Mork)
spells.flavour: In response to the Shaman's frantic hopping and gesticulating, a great green fist materialises from the air and wallops the foe!
spells.effect: Combat Place a large (5") blast template so that its central hole is directly over the centre of a unit the caster is engaged in combat with. Once placed, the template will scatter D3"+1. Any model (friend or foe) whose base lies underneath the template's final position risks being hit and suffering a single Strength 4 hit with an AP of -1.

spells.name: Vindictive Glare
spells.flavour: Their brows furrowed and their teeth clenched, the Shaman's glare is so intense that their enemies visibly wither.
spells.effect: The target enemy unit suffers a single Strength 7 hit with the Multiple Wounds (D3) special rule and with no armour save permitted ( Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal). This spell may target an enemy unit engaged in combat.

spells.name: Hand of Mork (or Gork)
spells.flavour: A huge green hand appears from above and, with great care, flicks a hero across the battlefield.
spells.effect: This spell can only target friendly characters, but may target characters engaged in combat. You may immediately remove the target friendly character from the battlefield and replace it anywhere within 2D6" of its original location, but not within 3" of any enemy models. However, if a double 1 is rolled, the character lands badly and loses a single Wound. Note that this spell allows a character to leave combat.

spells.name: Bad Moon Rizin'
spells.flavour: The Shaman summons a great pale moon above the battlefield. Trembling under its baleful gaze, the enemy can barely lift their weapons!
spells.effect: Until the end of this turn, the target enemy unit suffers a -D3 modifier to its Weapon Skill and Initiative characteristics (to a minimum of 1).

spells.name: Evil Sun Shinin'
spells.flavour: The sun is out and there's an enemy army over there – it's a great day for a battle! 'Ave at it Boyz!
spells.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, friendly units that are within the caster's Command range may re-roll any rolls To Hit of a natural 1, and improve the Armour Piercing characteristic of their weapons by 1.

spells.name: 'Ere We Go!
spells.flavour: The Shaman begins a simple chant which is quickly and enthusiastically taken up by their friends.
spells.effect: Any friendly unit that is within the caster's Command range during the Declare Charges & Charge Reactions sub-phase of this turn increases its maximum possible charge range by 3" and, when it makes a Charge roll, may apply a +D3 modifier to the result.

spells.name: Foot of Gork (or Mork)
spells.flavour: There's a massive green foot on the battlefield and it seems to be moving!
spells.effect: Remains in Play . Place a large (5") blast template so that its central hole is within 15" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template is treated as difficult terrain . The template scatters 2D6" during every Start of Turn sub-phase. Any unit (friend or foe) that moves through the template, or that the template moves over, suffers D3+3 Strength 5 hits, each with an AP of -1. , Previous - Necromancy
